title: lepisanthes tetraphylla var. tetraphylla
intro: Lepisanthes tetraphylla var. tetraphylla, also known as the Four-leafed Lepisanthes, is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lepisanthes tetraphylla var. tetraphylla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
